# Auth Requirements Checklist: Cookie Auth Migration

- **Purpose**: Validate authentication, session, gateway, and security requirement quality before implementation planning
- **Created**: 2026-05-25
- **Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `$speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are frontend ownership requirements complete for admin, seller, and buyer sign-in and registration, including apps where public registration is not intended? [Completeness, Spec FR-002, Spec FR-003, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK002 Are browser auth route requirements complete for login, registration, session refresh, and logout through versioned gateway account routes? [Completeness, Spec FR-017, Spec SC-007]
- [ ] CHK003 Are browser session creation, renewal, expiry, invalidation, and sign-out requirements documented as distinct lifecycle states? [Completeness, Spec FR-005, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-011]
- [ ] CHK004 Are downstream authorization context requirements complete enough to preserve identity, roles, claims, and account-state decisions? [Completeness, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-009]
- [ ] CHK005 Are old IdentityService UI entry point requirements complete for login, registration, logout, return URLs, app or client hints, and safe fallback routing? [Completeness, Spec FR-014, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK006 Are profile-projection timing requirements documented for successful registration and sign-in before the matching UserService profile is available? [Gap, Spec FR-006, Edge Cases]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "secure browser session" clarified with required cookie visibility, transport, scope, lifetime, and renewal expectations? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-005, Spec FR-010]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "clear, safe, user-actionable errors" defined for invalid credentials, duplicate registration, validation failures, locked accounts, inactive accounts, and app access denial? [Clarity, Spec FR-007, User Stories 1-2]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "shared platform session" defined with enough precision for cookie domain, app origins, gateway acceptance, and app-specific role restrictions? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-016, Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "state-changing browser request" defined so teams know which browser requests require the CSRF header? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-012]
- [ ] CHK011 Are frontend post-auth destination requirements precise for each app and role outcome rather than relying on "appropriate" routing? [Clarity, User Story 1, Spec SC-002]
- [ ] CHK012 Is session renewal failure behavior described consistently for expired sessions, invalid sessions, page reloads, second tabs, and form-in-progress scenarios? [Clarity, Spec FR-010, Edge Cases]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK013 Are requirements consistent between frontend-owned auth UI and the requirement that IdentityService remains the account, credential, and session source of truth? [Consistency, Spec FR-001, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK014 Are gateway account route requirements consistent with the API catalog's `/api/v1/accounts/**` ownership by IdentityService? [Consistency, Spec FR-017, API Catalog]
- [ ] CHK015 Are shared-session requirements consistent with role and account-state enforcement remaining app-specific and service-policy-specific? [Consistency, Spec FR-009, Spec FR-016]
- [ ] CHK016 Are registration requirements consistent with the existing IdentityUserCreated-to-UserService profile creation behavior and UserService ownership boundaries? [Consistency, Spec FR-006, Service Docs]
- [ ] CHK017 Are CSRF requirements consistent with successful cookie-authenticated requests continuing through the gateway to downstream services? [Consistency, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-012]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK018 Can the "without rendering an IdentityService-hosted UI page" outcome be objectively evaluated across all normal admin, seller, and buyer sign-in paths? [Measurability, Spec SC-001]
- [ ] CHK019 Is the 5-second post-login destination criterion scoped clearly enough for local development conditions, network assumptions, and app-specific role redirects? [Measurability, Spec SC-002]
- [ ] CHK020 Are success criteria defined for failed auth and registration paths, not only successful sign-in, registration, storage, gateway, sign-out, and redirect outcomes? [Gap, Spec SC-001 through Spec SC-010]
- [ ] CHK021 Are CSRF rejection criteria measurable without relying on downstream state-change inspection that may vary by service? [Measurability, Spec SC-008]
- [ ] CHK022 Are session renewal success criteria measurable for reload, near-expiry, and failed-renewal paths across all three frontend apps? [Measurability, Spec SC-010]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK023 Are primary sign-in, registration, protected API, and sign-out scenarios complete, clear, and independently valuable? [Coverage, User Stories 1-4]
- [ ] CHK024 Are alternate access scenarios covered for signing in through one app and using another app with different role rules? [Coverage, Spec FR-016, User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK025 Are exception scenarios covered for invalid credentials, duplicate email, validation failure, locked or inactive accounts, missing CSRF token, expired session, and failed renewal? [Coverage, User Stories 1-4, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK026 Are recovery requirements defined for session expiry during in-progress user input and failed refresh after reload? [Gap, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK027 Are requirements specified for authorization state changes after email verification or seller-role propagation, including when a fresh session or renewal is needed? [Coverage, Spec FR-013, Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK028 Are old IdentityService URL redirect requirements specified for unsafe or untrusted return URL values? [Gap, Spec FR-014]
- [ ] CHK029 Are requirements defined for a valid browser session whose underlying account becomes locked, inactive, deleted, or otherwise unauthorized after session creation? [Gap, Spec FR-009, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK030 Are requirements defined for concurrent tabs or multiple frontend apps sharing session renewal, logout, and signed-out state transitions? [Gap, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK031 Are requirements defined for browsers or environments where SameSite/Secure cookie behavior prevents the expected shared session? [Gap, Spec FR-012, Spec FR-016]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK032 Are cookie confidentiality, integrity, SameSite mode, Secure attribute, HttpOnly visibility, and refresh-token exposure requirements explicitly specified? [Security, Spec FR-005, Spec FR-012]
- [ ] CHK033 Are CSRF token issuance, rotation, validation failure, and custom header requirements specified with enough detail for consistent enforcement? [Security, Spec FR-012, Spec SC-008]
- [ ] CHK034 Are observability requirements documented for authentication failures, CSRF rejections, gateway session extraction failures, and downstream authorization denials? [Gap, Non-Functional]
- [ ] CHK035 Are accessibility and localization requirements specified for the new frontend-owned login, registration, error, and signed-out states? [Gap, Non-Functional]
- [ ] CHK036 Are privacy requirements documented for avoiding sensitive account detail disclosure in auth errors and logs? [Security, Spec FR-007]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK037 Are assumptions about IdentityService authority endpoints versus gateway account routes documented without creating conflicting browser auth paths? [Assumption, Spec FR-017, API Catalog]
- [ ] CHK038 Are dependencies between IdentityService, ApiGateway, frontend shared auth code, and downstream service policy behavior identified before planning? [Dependency, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-017]
- [ ] CHK039 Are out-of-scope items such as social or external login, admin account creation, and direct OIDC protocol endpoints clearly bounded? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK040 Are service ownership boundaries documented for IdentityService session issuance, ApiGateway forwarding, UserService profiles, and downstream authorization decisions? [Dependency, Service Docs]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK041 Is any conflict between "IdentityService no longer owns UI pages" and existing IdentityServer interactive account pages explicitly resolved for legacy URLs and protocol-required pages? [Conflict, Spec FR-001, Spec FR-014]
- [ ] CHK042 Is the requirements language clear enough to distinguish browser cookies from downstream bearer authorization context? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-005, Spec FR-008]
- [ ] CHK043 Are requirements explicit about whether logout is shared across all apps and all tabs for the platform session? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-016]
- [ ] CHK044 Is the boundary between user-facing requirements and implementation details preserved where gateway token extraction and forwarding are mentioned? [Consistency, Spec FR-008]

## Notes

- Generated from `spec.md` only because `plan.md` and `tasks.md` are not present yet.
- This checklist evaluates requirement quality. It is not an implementation test plan.
